revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''滝之城横穴墓群'''（たきのじょうおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[所沢市]]にある[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]]である。 所沢...'
revision.text:
'''滝之城横穴墓群'''（たきのじょうおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[所沢市]]にある[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]]である。
所沢台地の東端、柳瀬川に面した急峻な南斜面上部に位置する。台地上にある滝之城跡の土塁防護工事中に9基の横穴墓が発見され、うち5基で調査が行われた。7世紀中頃の築造とみられ、1号墓と4号墓から2体ずつ遺骸が発掘されている。昭和51年市の史跡に指定された。
== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 北足立・入間』 さきたま出版会 2004年
==関連事項== *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきのしようよこあなほくん}} [[Category:群集墳]] [[Category:埼玉県の歴史]] [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:所沢市]]

'''滝之城横穴墓群'''（たきのじょうおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[所沢市]]にある[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]]である。
所沢台地の東端、[[柳瀬川]]に面した急峻な南斜面上部に位置する。台地上にある[[滝の城|滝之城]]跡の[[土塁]]防護工事中に9基の[[横穴墓]]が発見され、うち5基で調査が行われた。[[7世紀]]中頃の築造とみられ、1号墓と4号墓から2体ずつ遺骸が発掘されている。1976年(昭和51年)に市の史跡に指定された。
== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 北足立・入間』 さきたま出版会、2004年
==関連事項== *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417/index.html 滝之城横穴墓群] - 所沢市公式サイト 2010年8月20日
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきのしようよこあなほくん}} [[Category:群集墳]] [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]]

'''滝之城横穴墓群'''（たきのじょうおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[所沢市]]にある[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]]である。
所沢台地の東端、[[柳瀬川]]に面した急峻な南斜面上部に位置する。台地上にある[[滝の城|滝之城]]跡の[[土塁]]防護工事中に9基の[[横穴墓]]が発見され、うち5基で調査が行われた。[[7世紀]]中頃の築造とみられ、1号墓と4号墓から2体ずつ遺骸が発掘されている。1976年(昭和51年)に市の史跡に指定された。
== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 北足立・入間』 さきたま出版会、2004年
==関連事項== *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417/index.html 滝之城横穴墓群] - 所沢市公式サイト 2010年8月20日
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきのしようよこあなほくん}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:群集墳]] [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]]

'''滝之城横穴墓群'''（たきのじょうおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[所沢市]]にある[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]]である。
[[武蔵野台地]]の一部である所沢台地の東端、[[柳瀬川]]に面した急峻な南斜面上部に位置する。台地上にある[[滝の城|滝之城]]跡の[[土塁]]防護工事中に9基の[[横穴墓]]が発見され、うち5基で調査が行われた。[[7世紀]]中頃の築造とみられ、1号墓と4号墓から2体ずつ遺骸が発掘されている。1976年(昭和51年)に市の史跡に指定された。
== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 北足立・入間』 さきたま出版会、2004年
== 関連事項 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]] * [[群集墳]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417/index.html 滝之城横穴墓群] - 所沢市公式サイト 2010年8月20日
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|48|1.2|N|139|31|55.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきのしようよこあなほくん}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:群集墳]] [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]]

'''滝之城横穴墓群'''（たきのじょうおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[所沢市]]にある[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]]である。
[[武蔵野台地]]の一部である所沢台地の東端、[[柳瀬川]]に面した急峻な南斜面上部に位置する。台地上にある[[滝の城|滝之城]]跡の[[土塁]]防護工事中に9基の[[横穴墓]]が発見され、うち5基で調査が行われた。横穴墓の羨道手前の横穴は粘土や丸礫や砂質泥岩で封鎖され、羨道と玄室の間は礫が敷き詰められた床で仕切られている<ref name="chinei"/>。[[7世紀]]中頃の築造とみられ、1号墓と4号墓から2体ずつ遺骸が発掘されている。また、[[須恵器]]のほか[[金環]]や小玉も出土している<ref name="chinei">『角川日本地名大辞典 11 埼玉県』1082頁。</ref>。9基ある横穴墓のほかにもまだ未発掘の横穴墓も多数ある<ref name="chinei"/>。[[1976年]]（昭和51年）4月1日に市の史跡に指定された。
== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 北足立・入間』 さきたま出版会、2004年 * {{Cite book|和書 |author =「角川日本地名大辞典」編纂委員会 |year = 1980-07-08 |title =[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 11 埼玉県 |publisher =角川書店 |page =1082 |isbn =4040011104 }}
== 関連事項 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]] * [[群集墳]] * [[北秋津横穴墓群]] - 柳瀬川流域にある同様の遺跡

{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|48|1.2|N|139|31|55.9|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきのしようよこあなほくん}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:群集墳]] [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]]

'''滝之城横穴墓群'''（たきのじょうおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[所沢市]]にある[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]]である。
[[武蔵野台地]]の一部である所沢台地の東端、[[柳瀬川]]に面した急峻な南斜面上部に位置する。台地上にある[[滝の城|滝之城]]跡の[[土塁]]防護工事中に9基の[[横穴墓]]が発見され、うち5基で調査が行われた。横穴墓の羨道手前の横穴は粘土や丸礫や砂質泥岩で封鎖され、羨道と玄室の間は礫が敷き詰められた床で仕切られている<ref name="chinei"/>。[[7世紀]]中頃の築造とみられ、1号墓と4号墓から2体ずつ遺骸が発掘されている。また、[[須恵器]]のほか[[金環]]や小玉も出土している<ref name="chinei">『角川日本地名大辞典 11 埼玉県』1082頁。</ref>。9基ある横穴墓のほかにもまだ未発掘の横穴墓も多数ある<ref name="chinei"/>。[[1976年]]（昭和51年）4月1日に市の史跡に指定された。

revision.comment: Created page with " == Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. === Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site..."
revision.text:
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|茂ヶ崎横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宗禅寺横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoguchi|ja|清戸迫横穴墓}} ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|ja|双葉町}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoguchi|ja|泉崎横穴墓}} ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|ja|西白河郡}}, {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|ja|泉崎村}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakada Yokoguchi|ja|中田横穴}} ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Yokoguchi|ja|羽山横穴}} ({{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|和田大仏及び横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sukagawa|ja|須賀川市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十五郎穴横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|ja|ひたちなか市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karagoshō Yokoguchi|ja|唐御所横穴}} ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoguchi Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} ({{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakka|ja|吉見百穴}} ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|ja|吉見町}}, [[Saitama Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|黒岩横穴墓群}} (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十郎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|ja|鳩山町}}, Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|比丘尼山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高根横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|長柄横穴群}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|ja|長柄町}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|出山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|谷沢川横穴群}} ({{ill|Setagaya|ja|世田谷区}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|荏子田横穴}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|ja|青葉区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|市ヶ尾横穴古墳群}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|柏谷横穴群}} ({{ill|Kannami|ja|函南町}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北江間横穴群}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|ja|伊豆の国市}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|江浦横穴群}} ({{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|ja|法皇山横穴古墳}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|安福寺横穴群}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十王免横穴群}} ({{ill|Matsue|ja|松江市}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|狐谷横穴墓群}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|上塩冶横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|ja|出雲市}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高津久横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|ja|知夫村}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|ja|竹並遺跡}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|ja|行橋市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|城山横穴群}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|ja|福智町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|ja|水町遺跡}} ({{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|ja|古月横穴}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|ja|鞍手町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫ナギノ横穴群}} ({{ill|Tamana|ja|玉名市}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫穴観音横穴群}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|鍋田横穴群}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|ja|山鹿市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|大村横穴群}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|ja|人吉市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|四日市横穴群}} ({{ill|Usa|ja|宇佐市}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|滝尾百穴横穴古墳群}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|ja|大分市}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|蓮ヶ池横穴群}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|ja|宮崎市}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, aa) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|比丘尼山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高根横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|長柄横穴群}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|ja|長柄町}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|出山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|谷沢川横穴群}} ({{ill|Setagaya|ja|世田谷区}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|荏子田横穴}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|ja|青葉区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|市ヶ尾横穴古墳群}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|柏谷横穴群}} ({{ill|Kannami|ja|函南町}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北江間横穴群}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|ja|伊豆の国市}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|江浦横穴群}} ({{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|ja|法皇山横穴古墳}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|安福寺横穴群}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十王免横穴群}} ({{ill|Matsue|ja|松江市}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|狐谷横穴墓群}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|上塩冶横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|ja|出雲市}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高津久横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|ja|知夫村}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|ja|竹並遺跡}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|ja|行橋市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|城山横穴群}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|ja|福智町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|ja|水町遺跡}} ({{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|ja|古月横穴}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|ja|鞍手町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫ナギノ横穴群}} ({{ill|Tamana|ja|玉名市}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫穴観音横穴群}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|鍋田横穴群}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|ja|山鹿市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|大村横穴群}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|ja|人吉市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|四日市横穴群}} ({{ill|Usa|ja|宇佐市}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|滝尾百穴横穴古墳群}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|ja|大分市}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|蓮ヶ池横穴群}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|ja|宮崎市}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.

revision.text:
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, aa) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en}}[[ja:所沢市]], Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.
*

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, aa) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en}}[[ja:所沢市]], Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Clustered burial mounds|ja|群集墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: changing link to [[Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster]]
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Clustered burial mounds|ja|群集墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

{{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

{{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Clustered burial mounds|ja|群集墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. '''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. '''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴墓]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴墓]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Clustered burial mounds|ja|群集墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴墓]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴墓]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴墓]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴墓]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.

The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴墓]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴墓]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Clustered burial mounds|ja|群集墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Clustered burial mounds]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa City]]
[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]]
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, aa) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en}}[[ja:所沢市]], Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Clustered burial mounds|ja|群集墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)
