'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.

The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Clustered burial mounds|ja|群集墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)

The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Yokouana Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.

The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster" to "Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster"
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Clustered burial mounds|ja|群集墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)

== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, aa) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en}}[[ja:所沢市]], Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104.== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)

== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, aa) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en}}[[ja:所沢市]], Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also! == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Clustered Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa City]]
[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]]
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, aa) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en}}[[ja:所沢市]], Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)

=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}} ({{ill|Kannami|en}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Numazu|en}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|12=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|12=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|12=draft}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|12=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|12=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|12=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|12=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
