revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Return the see also]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|12=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|12=draft}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, aa) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en|12=draft}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|12=draft}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=draft}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=draft}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kannami|en}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Numazu|en}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=draft}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tamana|en}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Usa|en|12=draft}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|12=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|12=draft}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|12=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|12=draft}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, aa) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en|12=draft}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|12=draft}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=draft}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=draft}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kannami|en}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Numazu|en}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=draft}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tamana|en}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Usa|en|12=draft}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.
*

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|12=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

[[sn:akinojō Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[en:Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group}} {{Kofun navbar}}

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|12=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

[[sn:akinojō Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[en:Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group}} {{Kofun navbar}}

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|12=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|12=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|12=draft|12=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|12=draft|12=draft}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, aa) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kannami|en}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Numazu|en}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tamana|en}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Usa|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.
*

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)

The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. <!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--> <!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--> <!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--> <!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--> {{translated page|ja|滝之城横穴墓群|version=70276050|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, aa) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kannami|en}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Numazu|en}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|1=Hōōzan Cave Tombs|2=ja|3=法皇山横穴古墳|4=fr|5=Tombeaux troglodytes de Hōōzan|6=simple|7=Hōōzan Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tamana|en}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Usa|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.
*

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojo Castle|qq=}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojo Castle|qq=}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojo Castle|qq=}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster]] → [[Deyama Graves]]
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojo Castle|qq=}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojo Castle|qq=}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Takinojo Castle|ja|滝の城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojo Castle|qq=}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:埼玉県の古墳
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.

Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara, Chiba|qq=}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}

<!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:埼玉県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]]

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Bot: replace redirect category → Kofun of Saitama prefecture
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:埼玉県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:群集墳]]

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, aa) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa|qq=}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashimatsuyama|qq=}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya|qq=}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara, Chiba|qq=}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Graves|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitaka, Tokyo|qq=}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama|qq=}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
