revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Municipal Historic Sites of Saitama Prefecture
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. <!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:埼玉県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:群集墳]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:埼玉県内の市町村指定史跡]] into [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Saitama Prefecture]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[sue ware]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshufun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|qq=}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|qq=}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.

9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashimatsuyama|qq=}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya|qq=}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara, Chiba|qq=}}, [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]) - {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.comment: Bot: sync Kofun‑gun infobox from Wikidata (image/location/coords/etc.)
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|qid=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|qid=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|qid=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|qid=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|qid=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q17209840}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|1=Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11403899}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q17209840}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
