revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of [[:en:Taki-no Castle|Takinojo Castle]], nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s [[:en:dromos|dromos]] was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, [[:en:1976|1976]], the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Taki-no Castle|en|Taki-no Castle|WD=Q2970751|lt=Takinojo Castle}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s {{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos}} was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}}, the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Akitsu Cave Tomb Tomb Group|1=Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11403899}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, aa) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa|qq=}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashimatsuyama|qq=}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya|qq=}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Graves|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitaka, Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Taki-no Castle|en|Taki-no Castle|WD=Q2970751|lt=Takinojo Castle}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s {{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos}} was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}}, the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Taki-no Castle|en|Taki-no Castle|WD=Q2970751|lt=Takinojo Castle}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s {{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos}} was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}}, the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209840]] Wikidata name: Taki-no-jō Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Taki-no Castle|en|Taki-no Castle|WD=Q2970751|lt=Takinojo Castle}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s {{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos}} was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}}, the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}} '''Takinojo Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Taki-no Castle|en|Taki-no Castle|WD=Q2970751|lt=Takinojo Castle}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s {{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos}} was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}}, the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Taki-no Castle|en|Taki-no Castle|WD=Q2970751|lt=Takinojo Castle}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s {{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos}} was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}}, the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}} '''Takinojo Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Taki-no Castle|en|Taki-no Castle|WD=Q2970751|lt=Takinojo Castle}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s {{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos}} was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}}, the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}} '''Takinojo Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Taki-no Castle|en|Taki-no Castle|WD=Q2970751|lt=Takinojo Castle}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s {{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos}} was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}}, the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshūfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group|1=Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabogun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11403899}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Yokoanabo Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)
== Distribution and Notable Yokoanabo Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, aa) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojō Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa|qq=}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaakitsu Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bikuniyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashimatsuyama|qq=}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya|qq=}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}} '''Takinojo Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Taki-no Castle|en|Taki-no Castle|WD=Q2970751|lt=Takinojo Castle|1=Taki-no Castle}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s {{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos|1=Dromos}} was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}}, the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}} '''Takinojo Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Taki-no Castle|en|Taki-no Castle|WD=Q2970751|lt=Takinojo Castle|1=Taki-no Castle}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s {{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos|1=Dromos}} was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}}, the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}} '''Takinojo Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Taki-no Castle|en|Taki-no Castle|WD=Q2970751|lt=Takinojo Castle|1=Taki-no Castle}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s {{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos|1=Dromos}} was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}}, the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.800333|N|139.532194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=0700}} '''Takinojo Yokoanabo Tomb Group''' (滝之城横穴墓群) is a collection of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Tokorozawa City, Saitama Prefecture]].
The tombs are situated on the upper portion of a steep southern slope facing the [[Yanase River (Saitama)|Yanase River]], at the eastern edge of the Tokorozawa Plateau, which is part of the [[Musashino Plateau]]. During protective embankment work at the ruins of {{ill|Taki-no Castle|en|Taki-no Castle|WD=Q2970751|lt=Takinojo Castle|1=Taki-no Castle}}, nine rock-cut tombs were discovered. Five of these tombs were excavated and studied. The entrance to each tomb’s {{ill|dromos|en|dromos|WD=Q1222967|lt=dromos|1=Dromos}} was sealed with clay, rounded pebbles, and sandy mudstone, while the passage leading to the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was separated by a floor paved with stones<ref name="chinei"/>.
The tombs are believed to have been built around the [[mid-7th century]]. Excavations revealed two skeletons each in Tomb No. 1 and Tomb No. 4. Artifacts unearthed include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, gold earrings, and small beads<ref name="chinei">'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture' p. 1082.</ref>. In addition to the nine known tombs, it is believed that many other unexcavated tombs remain in the area<ref name="chinei"/>.
On April 1, {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}}, the site was designated as a Historic Site of Tokorozawa City.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shiono Hiroshi, 'Saitama no Kofun: Kita Adachi and Iruma,' Sakitama Publishing, September 2004, pp. 544-547. ISBN 978-4878913815. * Editorial Committee of 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten,' 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 11: Saitama Prefecture,' Kadokawa Shoten, July 8, 1980, p. 1082. ISBN 4040011104. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Saitama Prefecture|1=list of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}} * {{ill|Clustered Kofuns|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clustered Kofuns|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshūfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}} * {{ill|Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group|ja|北秋津横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Akitsu Yokoanabo Tomb Group|1=Kitaakitsu Yokoanabo Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11403899}} - A similar archaeological site along the Yanase River.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/siseki/bunzai_20100318120005417.html Takinojo Yokoanabo Tomb Group] - Tokorozawa City Official Website (August 20, 2010)

Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) -
